title: Xtra Bitter Ethiopian Apricot Dabo
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish with a twist, featuring bitter notes and fluffy texture, perfect for a solo dinner, this dabo incorporates apricot and rest-meat for a unique flavor profile, and is suitable for those who prefer raw-friendly ingredients.

ingredients.name: rest-meat
ingredients.notes: such as beef or lamb, finely chopped

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine teff flour, water, and active dry yeast, stirring until a smooth batter forms.
Let the mixture rest in a warm place for 24 hours to allow fermentation.
Add chopped dried apricot, rest-meat, berbere spice, salt, and mitmita to the batter, stirring until well combined.
Knead the mixture for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Shape the mixture into a round dabo shape and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and cook the dabo for 5 minutes on each side, until it is golden brown and cooked through.
Remove the dabo from the skillet and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the dabo warm, garnished with additional chopped dried apricot if desired.
